data.title: Neo Performance announces normal course issuer bid for up to 3.30M shares
data.description:
Building blocks manufacturer Neo Performance Materials (OTCPK:NOPMF) (TSX:NEO:CA) intends to make a normal course issuer bid for up to 3.30M of its issued and outstanding common shares.

The company has entered into an automatic share purchase plan with its designated broker to allow for the repurchases.

The program is expected to commence on June 11, 2025, and terminate on June 10, 2026.

The repurchases will be made in the open market through the facilities of the Toronto Stock Exchange or alternative Canadian trading systems.

As of May 30, there were 41.82M shares issued and outstanding.

data.title: Shopify is called an underappreciated AI play
data.description:
Wells Fargo singled out Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) as an underappreciated AI play in a note published on Friday.

Analyst Andrew Bauch said the firm views Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) as one of the names in the Fintech universe that can be seen as a thematic AI story. The AI thesis on Shopify (SHOP) was based on the company's staunch commitment to leveraging AI internally, its differentiated AI-powered merchant solutions, and growing partnerships with the most important AI names in the industry, such as OpenAI, META Platforms (META), and Perplexity. Bucking the theory that the AI wave is a threat to Shopify (SHOP), Wells Fargo said it sees layers of opportunity for the Canadian company via use cases, efficiencies, and adoption.

Shopify (SHOP) is seen as an obvious beneficiary of the AI wave pushing even more retail sales to e-commerce channels. 

"We believe SHOP's strong product lineup and strategic partnerships in AI position the company to capture a similar share in agentic commerce (low to mid-teens) as traditional U.S. eComm (high teens). Given our projected $505B GMV opportunity for agentic commerce by 2030 (growing ~30% from today annually, similar to early days eComm), we estimate ~12.5% market share could drive MSD-HSD upside to SHOP's GMV vs. current Street estimates."

Wells Fargo kept an Overweight rating on Shopify (SHOP) in place and boosted its price target to $125 from $107.

Shares of Shopify (SHOP) edged 0.7% higher in premarket trading on Friday to $105.03 vs. the 52-week range of $48.56 to $129.38.

data.title: Bowman Consulting Group announces $25M share buyback program
data.description:
• Bowman Consulting Group (NASDAQ:BWMN) announced a new share repurchase program under which BWMN may repurchase up to $25M of its common stock over the next 12 months commencing June 9, 2025.
  • This new authorization replaces BWMN’s stock repurchase authorization, which terminates on June 6, 2025. 
  • BWMN shares up 1.7% premarket on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Quarterhill appoints David Charron as permanent CFO
SA Graphics: Full timeline of the Musk-Trump feud
data.description:
Marking the EV maker's worst session on record, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) crashed 14.3% to $284.70/share on Thursday to erase $152B in market cap. The stock rebounded 5% overnight, but the price is near the same level at which it was trading around the presidential election on Nov. 5, 2024.

Translation: Tesla has now surrendered most of the gains garnered since the so-called "Trump bump." And for good reason. CEO Elon Musk and President Trump have had a very bad breakup, with escalating rhetoric continuing to spill out into the open.

Things have reportedly gone sour in the West Wing for a while, where Musk served as the head of the newly created Department of Government Efficiency. His agenda at DOGE oftentimes clashed with the administration's efforts, with Musk getting into fights with Scott Bessent, Marco Rubio, Sean Duffy and Peter Navarro. Musk was also approaching the end of his term as special advisor with a need to spend more time at Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX (SPACE), at the same time that Trump was pushing his "Big Beautiful Bill" through Congress - to the dismay of Elon's mission to bring financial accountability to the government.

That wasn't all. While the "BBB" included tax incentives for the oil and gas industry, it slashed EV credits and imposed a fee on EV owners in place of the federal gas tax. That appeared to be the final straw for Musk (along with missing out on his preferred NASA pick), after donating millions of dollars to Trump's election campaign, helping shore up the voter base, and standing by his side while seeing his companies spiral during his time at the White House. On the other side of the Resolute Desk, Trump was committed to DOGE, but had to balance an entire administration (not just one newly created department), with an extremely narrow majority in Congress and a need to make concessions along spending lines.

Take a step back: The war of words has since escalated over X, Truth Social, and in the Oval Office (see a graphic on the back-and-forth below). The public brawl has covered accusations that involve Jeffrey Epstein, potential termination of government contracts, and threats to decommission SpaceX vehicles. Reports suggest that White House aides have scheduled a call for today between Trump and Musk, while billionaire Bill Ackman urged the sides to "make peace for the benefit of our country," to which Musk replied, "you're not wrong."

data.title: Strategy prices STRD preferred IPO at $85/share
data.description:
Strategy's (NASDAQ:MSTR) initial public offering of 11.76M shares of 10.00% series A perpetual stride preferred stock (STRD) priced at $85.00 per share, the company said Friday.

The issuance and sale of the STRD shares are scheduled to settle on June 10, 2025.

The company estimates the IPO's net proceeds will amount to $979.7M, which will be used for general corporate purposes including bitcoin purchases.

As of June 1, 2025, Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) held 580,955 bitcoins (BTC-USD).

The STRD shares are the third series of preferred stock Strategy (MSTR) has issued in its strategy to issue securities to raise capital to add to bitcoin (BTC-USD) holdings on its balance sheet. Its other series of preferred stock include STRK and STRF.

Dividends on the STRD stock won't be mandatory, the company said. Holders of the shares will be entitled to receive non-cumulative dividends at a rate per year equal to 10.00% of the stated amount when, as, and if declared by the company's board.

Strategy (MSTR) stock rose 1.7% in Friday premarket trading. That's as bitcoin (BTC-USD) slipped 0.7% to $103.9K.

data.title: European markets mostly lower as investors await global trade clues
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.15am ET. 

London (UKX) +0.01% to 8,811. The Halifax House Price Index in the UK increased 2.5% Y/Y in May.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.19% to 24,279. Germany's exports drop in April, denting trade surplus. Industrial production fell by 1.4% M/M in April.

France (CAC:IND) -0.04% to 7,787. France trade deficit widens more than expected in April. Industrial production in France unexpectedly declined by 1.4% M/M.

Euro Area inflation rate eases to 1.90% in May.

The Czech Republic’s trade surplus narrowed to CZK 23.2 billion in April.

Norway’s manufacturing production rose by 2.8% M/M in April.

Denmark’s manufacturing production rose by 2% M/M in April.

Retail sales in Slovakia fell by 0.4% Y/Y in April.

Retail sales in Hungary climbed by 5% Y/Y in April.

Industrial Production in Hungary fell by 5% Y/Y in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) almost flat at 551.90, as investors weighed global trade sentiment. Traders adopted a cautious stance ahead of further developments in trade talks between the U.S. and China. 

In Europe, the ECB cut rates by 25 bps on Thursday and downgraded its inflation outlook for 2025 and 2026, reinforcing the case for a more accommodative stance.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 5 basis points to 2.54%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis points to 4.62%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: DocuSign 'dilemma' comes to light as Wall Street frets over future
data.description:
DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU) was in focus on Friday after the electronic signature company lowered its full-year billings guidance, causing Wall Street analysts to express some concern about its near-term future.

Shares plunged 18.5% in premarket trading.

“DOCU's dilemma is the [near-term] disconnect between results/KPIs with [management's] tone & [intelligent agreement management] bullishness,” Morgan Stanley analyst Josh Baer wrote in a note to clients. “It may be timing, in which case patient investors will be rewarded.”

Baer, who has an Equal-Weight rating on DocuSign, lowered his price target to $86 from $92, said more evidence is needed that the bullishness for intelligent agreement management is real.

“For the neutral, less-patient investor who is waiting for more evidence IAM can clearly be incremental to the model, staying on the sidelines for the next quarter makes sense, unlikely to miss a material move higher until 2HFY26 at the earliest,” Baer wrote.

He continued: “In our view, we side closest with the neutral investor – and given some of the bear case concerns, we are waiting for more evidence (to show up in our primary checks/diligence, results/KPIs) before underwriting the bullish IAM-driven growth acceleration to double digits – and therefore, we remain [Equal Weight].”

Evercore ISI analyst Kirk Materne said DocuSign is “moving in the right direction,” but the billings weakness is an issue investors will have to contend with for a while.

“While the billings miss and guide down is weighing on the stock this morning, management seems comfortable with the overall trends in the business and views the billings shortfall as more of an unforced error on their part — in terms of not de-risking for less early renewals in F1Q — vs. a fundamental shift in the business,” Materne wrote.

“In general, we believe the reaction today is perhaps a little overdone if taking a longer-term view, but until the Street builds greater confidence in the adjusted outlook, shares could remain in a bit of a trading range.”

data.title: Global gold ETF flows turn negative in May, total outflows hit $1.8 billion
data.description:
Global physically backed gold ETFs lost $1.8 billion in May, snapping their five-month inflow streak.

North America led the decline with $1.5 billion in outflows, followed by Asia with $489 million, while Europe bucked the trend, recording $225 million in inflows, the World Gold Council said. 

The first monthly outflow since last November and a mild fall in the gold price also saw global gold ETFs’ total assets under management (AUM) fall 1% to $374 billion. 

The better-than-expected temporary easing of tariffs between the US and China improved investor risk appetite, which led to a strong rebound in equities, but lower safe-haven demand for gold.

Gold prices ended the month of May almost flat (-0.7%) at $3,278 an ounce, albeit with some intra-month volatility. Year-to-date, gold remains up a staggering 26%.

The US Fed kept rates steady at its May meeting; minutes showed a cautious stance towards rate decisions amid an outlook of persistent inflation and risks to the labour market.

As a result, the market is now expecting higher rates by the end of 2025, leading to rising US Treasury yields and increasing the opportunity cost of holding gold. 

Gold market trading volumes averaged $363 billion/day in May, 18% lower m/m yet significantly higher than the 2024 average of $233 billion/day.

Total net longs of COMEX gold futures meanwhile reached 551t by the end of May, a mild 3% decline m/m. 

More Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), (DUST)
